channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Bharti Airtel Profit Falls 33.5% Despite Revenue Surge
item.description: Bharti Airtel posted a 33.5% year-on-year drop in net profit to ₹7,325 crore for Q4 FY26. This came despite consolidated revenue growing 15.7% to ₹55,383 crore and EBIT rising 21.4%. India's ARPU hit ₹257. The company recommended a ₹24 per share dividend. Investments in data centers and network expansion continue, even as profit faced challenges. The stock rose 1.84% on the news.

item.title: Uber Partners Adani to Build Global Tech Hub in India
item.description: Uber announced its first Indian data center, a collaboration with the Adani Group to be operational by year-end 2026. This move leverages India's expanding digital infrastructure and talent pool to build technology "at scale, from India, for the world." The partnership aligns with Adani's $100 billion commitment to AI-ready data centers and taps into India's growing role as a global technology hub amid surging AI and cloud demand.

item.title: Experion's Rs 4,000 Cr Bookings Show Luxury Real Estate Boom
item.description: Experion Developers announced a record Rs 4,000 crore in bookings for fiscal year 2026, nearly doubling its previous year's performance. This surge was fueled by luxury residential projects in Gurugram and Noida, notably the SAATORI development which alone sold Rs 1,800 crore. The results highlight a robust luxury real estate market driven by high-net-worth individuals and limited supply, though concerns about market saturation and economic sensitivity persist.

item.title: PFC-REC Merger Faces Regulatory Risk As Profits Dip
item.description: Power Finance Corporation (PFC) posted a 3% rise in net profit to ₹8,597.61 crore for the quarter. However, this profit growth hides a drop in total income to ₹28,856.60 crore and an 11% fall in net interest income to ₹10,833 crore. PFC plans to merge with REC Ltd by April 1, 2027, but faces major regulatory challenges due to the RBI's new rules for top-tier NBFCs. The company also saw ₹1,500 crore in currency-linked losses. PFC's P/E is about 6.39, REC's is around 5.81.

item.title: J&K Bank Posts Record ₹2,363 Cr Profit, Recovers from Loss
item.description: Jammu & Kashmir Bank (J&K Bank) achieved a record profit of approximately ₹2,363 crore for fiscal year 2026, a strong turnaround from a ₹1,200 crore loss in 2020. Key drivers include enhanced governance, a robust board, disciplined provisioning, and focused asset recovery, which helped reduce Gross Non-Performing Assets (NPA) to 2.5%. The bank navigates regional geopolitical risks and industry deposit challenges using its unique public-private hybrid model and customer engagement efforts.

item.title: Zydus Lifesciences Board to Decide Share Buyback May 19
item.description: Zydus Lifesciences will hold a board meeting on May 19, 2026, to consider a share buyback. The decision comes alongside the company's full-year financial results. This follows a small stock price increase and shifts in institutional holdings, with Foreign Investors reducing stakes and Domestic Investors increasing theirs. The company's valuation appears competitive within the pharmaceutical sector.

item.title: Axis MF Recommends Multicap Funds Amid Strong Equity Inflows
item.description: Axis Mutual Fund executive Vandana Trivedi suggests multicap funds are a good choice for investors looking for long-term value, offering a strong alternative to large-cap stocks. This comes as equity mutual funds, including flexicap, midcap, and smallcap funds, continue to attract significant investor money. Trivedi explains that multicap funds spread investments across different company sizes, helping capture growth in mid and small-cap companies. She also warns that more exposure to these smaller companies means higher risk and requires a long-term view with a strong tolerance for volatility.

item.title: Bharti Hexacom Profit Falls Amid Revenue Rise, Homes Segment Soars
item.description: Bharti Hexacom reported a 4.6% drop in net profit for Q4 FY26 to ₹446.7 crore, even as revenue climbed 5.4% to ₹2,414 crore. Operational efficiency improved, with EBITDA up 8.5% and margins hitting 53%. The homes, office, and other services segment was a major growth driver, up 65.3% year-on-year. Mobile ARPU rose to ₹252.

item.title: Copper-Gold Ratio Breakout Signals Bitcoin Rally, But What's Next?
item.description: The copper-to-gold ratio has climbed above its 200-day moving average, a signal historically preceding Bitcoin bull markets. Current prices place gold near $4,700/oz and copper at $6.65/lb. However, persistent inflation, delayed Federal Reserve rate cuts until late 2027, and Bitcoin's continued correlation with equities present a complex picture. While Bitcoin ETFs attract significant capital, a key institutional player has reduced exposure, highlighting underlying caution amidst elevated commodity prices and geopolitical uncertainty.
